enonce.texte: Read the direct speech sentences below. Apply the correct tense backshift and pronoun changes to transform them into reported speech. Remember the rules: Present Simple → Past Simple ; Present Continuous → Past Continuous ; Present Perfect → Past Perfect ; Will → Would.

questions.texte: Direct speech: "I love teaching English in Cameroon." What is the correct reported speech form?
questions.reponse: He said that he loved teaching English in Cameroon.

questions.texte: Direct speech: "We are preparing for the Probatoire examination." What is the correct reported speech form?
questions.reponse: They said that they were preparing for the Probatoire examination.

questions.texte: Direct speech: "I have just finished the module on conditionals." What is the correct reported speech form?
questions.reponse: She said that she had just finished the module on conditionals.

questions.texte: Direct speech: "We will visit the Foumban Royal Palace next week." What is the correct reported speech form?
questions.reponse: They said that they would visit the Foumban Royal Palace the following week.

enonce.texte: Context: During break time at a high school in Yaoundé, you hear several students talking. You need to report what they said to the Discipline Master. Transform the direct quotes into reported speech.
images: Cameroonian high school students chatting under a tree during break time, wearing blue and white uniforms

questions.texte: Student A says: "I don't like the new cafeteria food." (Report to the DM)
questions.reponse: Student A said that he/she didn't like the new cafeteria food.

questions.texte: Student B says: "The principal is checking the classrooms right now."
questions.reponse: Student B said that the principal was checking the classrooms then.

questions.texte: Student C says: "I have never seen such a difficult Maths test."
questions.reponse: Student C said that he/she had never seen such a difficult Maths test.

enonce.texte: You are a journalist for Cameroon Radio Television (CRTV). You interviewed a witness after a road accident on the Yaoundé-Douala highway. Transform the witness's exact words into reported speech for your news article.
images: Road accident scene on a highway with a damaged bus and an ambulance

questions.texte: Witness: "The bus driver was driving very fast when the tire burst."
questions.reponse: The witness reported that the bus driver had been driving very fast when the tire burst.

questions.texte: Witness: "I saw the accident with my own eyes."
questions.reponse: He said that he had seen the accident with his own eyes.

questions.texte: Witness: "People must respect the highway code."
questions.reponse: He stated that people must respect the highway code. (Note: 'Must' often remains 'must' for general obligations or rules, or changes to 'had to' for specific obligations. Here, general rule is kept).

enonce.texte: Your older brother left home 5 years ago. He just called and confessed several things to you. Later, you need to tell your parents exactly what he said. Use reported speech with the correct time and place changes (today → that day; here → there).
images: A young man talking on an old mobile phone looking guilty

questions.texte: Brother: "I broke my father's laptop before leaving."
questions.reponse: He admitted that he had broken my father's laptop before leaving.

questions.texte: Brother: "I am living in Douala now, but I will come home next month."
questions.reponse: He said that he was living in Douala then, but he would come home the following month.

questions.texte: Brother: "I have not forgotten the values you taught me."
questions.reponse: He told me that he had not forgotten the values we taught him.

enonce.texte: Analyze the following reported speech sentences written by a student. Identify the error (tense, pronoun, or adverb) and provide the corrected version.

questions.texte: Incorrect: She said that she is going to the market yesterday. (Correct it)
questions.reponse: She said that she was going to the market yesterday.

questions.texte: Incorrect: He said that he can help me tomorrow. (Reporting today)
questions.reponse: He said that he could help me the next day / the following day.

questions.texte: Incorrect: They said, "We will win the match" → They said that we will win the match. (Fix pronouns)
questions.reponse: They said that they would win the match.
